text.title: langchain-memory Failure Modes
text.summary: Five high-impact failure modes for LangChain memory in production. Each one has a concrete detector (something you can check programmatically) and a fix that maps back to a rule in 01-core-rules.xml.

antipattern.symptom: User A asks "what's my name?" and gets User B's name back.
antipattern.root-cause: session_id is a shared/static string, or derived from a predictable user attribute (email) that an attacker can replay. RunnableWithMessageHistory stores all messages under that key and the lookup leaks across users.
antipattern.fix: Generate session_id as a server-side UUID, store it in the user's signed cookie/JWT, never accept it from the client unverified. References rule r13.
antipattern.detector: Scan logs for >1 distinct user-id per session_id within 1h window — any hit is a leak.

antipattern.symptom: App works in dev, loses all conversations after each deploy.
antipattern.root-cause: get_session_history returns InMemoryChatMessageHistory backed by a process-local dict. Restart wipes the dict.
antipattern.fix: Swap to RedisChatMessageHistory or a Postgres-backed implementation; gate the in-memory variant behind ENV != production. References rule r1 (durability implication).
antipattern.detector: Restart the service mid-conversation; verify history survives. If not, you're in-memory.

antipattern.symptom: Token cost per turn grows linearly until you blow past the model context window around turn 200.
antipattern.root-cause: Buffer memory was kept past its sweet spot. No summarisation, no compaction, no swap to vector.
antipattern.fix: At expected_turns >10, swap to summary memory; at >50 or when recall is semantic, swap to vector memory. References rule r5 and r15.
antipattern.detector: Log token count per turn; plot it. Linear growth past turn 20 = misconfigured.

antipattern.symptom: Auditor asks "what did the user say in turn 12?" and you cannot retrieve it.
antipattern.root-cause: Summary memory summarised away the original text. Summary is lossy and irreversible.
antipattern.fix: Pair summary memory with an append-only raw-history log (separate Postgres table or S3) for audit. Summary serves the prompt; raw log serves compliance. References rule r2.
antipattern.detector: Try to retrieve a specific past turn by index; if you only have the summary, you've lost it.

antipattern.symptom: Vector memory returns turns from another user's session.
antipattern.root-cause: Shared vectorstore collection without session_id metadata filter. similarity_search ranks by semantic distance only and ignores tenant boundaries.
antipattern.fix: Add session_id (or user_id) to vectorstore metadata on every write; pass filter={"session_id": sid} on every read. References rule r3.
antipattern.detector: Insert a sentinel string in session A; query for it from session B; if returned, you have a leak.
